metadata.greeting: This weekend in London is packed with exciting and engaging activities for your 3-year-old! I've found several free museum options, like the Young V&A and the Natural History Museum, that offer interactive exhibits perfect for little ones. For outdoor fun, consider a visit to Golders Hill Park Zoo or one of London's fabulous playgrounds.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: The Young V&A offers sensory play and interactive exhibits designed for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: With hands-on exhibits and sensory elements, this museum is perfect for keeping a 3-year-old engaged.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station.
transport.tip: The museum is a short walk from the station.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: Victoria Park offers a variety of activities, including playgrounds, sports facilities, and green spaces for running around.
activities.whyItWorks: The park has plenty of open space for a 3-year-old to explore and burn off energy.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or walk to Victoria Park.
transport.tip: Check the park's website for any special events happening this weekend.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore historical homes and gardens through the ages.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum features hands-on exhibits and a lovely enclosed garden suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or train to Hoxton.
transport.tip: Pick up an activity sheet at the front desk to engage your child.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals to see and interact with.
activities.whyItWorks: A 3-year-old will enjoy seeing the animals up close.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney, easily accessible by bus or walking.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times to make the most of your visit.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: The playground is designed for toddlers and has a pond theme, which is great for sensory play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Located near the Olympic Park, so you can combine it with a visit there.

activities.title: Walthamstow Wetlands
activities.description: A large urban nature reserve with walking trails and wildlife.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to explore nature and see birds.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or train to Walthamstow.
transport.tip: Check the website for family events.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: An interactive story centre with indoor and outdoor play areas.
activities.whyItWorks: The centre is designed to spark imagination and creativity in children through storytelling and play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: A large park with playgrounds, fountains, and green spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: The park offers plenty of space for running around and exploring.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the website for events and activities.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the history of planet Earth with interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum has many interactive exhibits that are perfect for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Underground to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Visit the dinosaur exhibit early to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with hands-on exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum has a dedicated Wonderlab with hands-on experiences for kids.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Underground to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab in advance.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: The zoo is free and has a variety of animals that a 3-year-old will enjoy seeing.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Underground to Golders Green station.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A playground with a Peter Pan theme, including a pirate ship and sensory trail.
activities.whyItWorks: The playground is designed to be inclusive and offers a variety of play experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Underground to Notting Hill Gate or High Street Kensington station.
transport.tip: The playground can get crowded, so arrive early.

activities.title: Greenwich Park
activities.description: The oldest enclosed Royal Park in London with wide open spaces, a deer park, and playgrounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers open spaces for running around, a deer park to explore, and playgrounds.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Greenwich station.
transport.tip: Visit the Royal Observatory and stand on the Meridian Line.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: A large park with gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a children's zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities for children, including playgrounds and a mini zoo.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Underground to Sloane Square station, then a bus to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Visit the Battersea Park Children's Zoo.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities for children, including an adventure playground and a city farm.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Underground to Russell Square station.
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry if accompanying a child.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: The Horniman Museum features a variety of exhibits and a beautiful garden.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum offers free workshops for children, and the gardens include farm animals and a music laboratory.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill station.
transport.tip: Check for special events and activities on the museum's website.

activities.title: The Lion Inside
activities.description: A theatre show for children ages 3-7.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for the age group, should hold attention.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to London Bridge, then walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families, including shows, musical adventures, puppetry, and free activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of choice, something for everyone.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Check the schedule and book in advance for popular events.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and engaging, encourages imaginative play.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Part of the Spring Family Fun festival.

activities.title: Hamleys Fun Activities
activities.description: Fun activities at London's biggest toy shop.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic toy shop with demonstrations and activities to entertain children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Oxford Circus.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for specific events.

activities.title: London Zoo's Big Birthday Bash
activities.description: The Zoo is 200 years old! A family-friendly street party-style celebration with live music, party games, and a summer BBQ.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, music, and a party atmosphere.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail (if extended)
activities.dates: Check if extended to late April/May
activities.description: A mix of learning and chocolate motivation.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun and educational day out.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Check website for details.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Discover illustrated animals and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a walk in nature with a fun activity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or train to Walthamstow.
transport.tip: Check website for details.

activities.title: RAF Museum - Little Swifts
activities.description: Fun and lively sessions for pre-school children including arts and crafts, games, stories and songs all linked to the museums collection.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging activities for young children related to aviation.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Colindale.
transport.tip: Check website for dates and times.
